findings.summary: Phase XX-a and XX-b both declare src/auth.ts in their <files> writes block.
findings.remediation: Move src/auth.ts ownership to one phase (the other reads it), OR split into separate waves.
findings.why_real_risk: Parallel engineer agents in the same wave will both write src/auth.ts; the second commit silently overwrites the first because git apply has no merge semantics inside the worktree. The orchestrator's mechanical disjointness check (check-wave-disjointness.sh) would normally catch this, but the planner asserted disjointness in PLAN.md prose without verifying — the failure surfaces only at engineer dispatch.
findings.repro: Run /zapili:zapili against the current PLAN.md → Stage 7.0 disjointness check exits 1 with 'OVERLAP in Wave 1: PHASE-XX-a and PHASE-XX-b both write src/auth.ts'; workflow halts before any engineer is dispatched.
tests_to_add: tests/check-wave-disjointness/test_overlap_detected — assert that bash check-wave-disjointness.sh PLAN.md exits 1 when two phases in the same wave share a write target
tests/planner/test_no_silent_overlap — assert that planner output rejected when intra-wave writes intersect, before disjointness check runs

findings.summary: No contradictions between PLAN.md and CONTEXT.md detected.

not_fully_audited.reason: PHASE-XX-c.md references threat-model.md which was not provided in <inputs>; security review requires the referenced threat model to ground the analysis.
not_fully_audited.recommended_followup: Re-run codex-validate-plan.sh with the threat-model.md added as an additional <file role="context"> input.
